description_long: This is a classic adjustable pipe wrench featuring a robust metal head and a long, cylindrical wooden handle. The head is composed of two jaws, one fixed and one adjustable, with a knurled adjustment nut for altering the jaw width. The metal head is dark, likely made of steel or iron, and shows signs of use, with some areas appearing slightly worn. The handle is wooden, with a weathered, smooth finish and visible discoloration, suggesting frequent handling and age. The adjustment mechanism is exposed and metallic, with a blue section connecting the head to the handle. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements.
description: A well-used adjustable pipe wrench with a dark metal head and a weathered wooden handle, featuring a visible adjustment nut and signs of wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the jaws of the wrench and the adjustment nut, with the handle receding backward.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the wrench, the wooden handle, and the detailed adjustment mechanism.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden handle, with the head of the wrench less visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the other side of the wrench, with the adjustment nut and jaw details visible.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Visible wear and discoloration on the wooden handle, minor scuffing and dullness on the metal head, but no significant rust.
